title: Harbor Street Blackout
background: On the night of the Great Harbor Blackout — a staged citywide power failure lasting 47 minutes — forensic accountant Dana Morse vanished from her 11th-floor office at Pelican Analytics. Her encrypted laptop was found wiped, her coffee still warm, and a single red thread caught in the window latch. The blackout was not accidental. Dana had been building a fraud case against one of her firm's largest clients. Somebody knew she was close.

victim_profile.notes: Dana had been secretly compiling evidence of a $40M accounting fraud

timeline.detail: Dana stays behind citing 'deadline work'. Last confirmed sighting by receptionist.
Citywide blackout triggered by a fault at the Harbor substation. Later determined to be manually induced.
Lights return. Dana's office found empty. Coffee cup still warm at 9:50pm.
Dana's husband files report. Her phone last pinged a tower 4 blocks from the office at 9:02pm.
Laptop found wiped. Red thread on window latch. Elevator logs show Button 11 pressed at 9:01pm — from Basement.

true_motive: Richard Voss, CFO of Nexus Maritime, knew Dana had reconstructed the fraudulent accounts that would expose him and three board members to federal charges. He orchestrated the blackout to create cover, had her abducted during the darkness, and had her laptop wiped remotely.
true_method: Voss hired a private security contractor (a former contact from his military days) to trigger the substation failure remotely. The contractor entered Pelican Analytics via the basement loading dock during the blackout using a stolen access card, disabled the elevator cameras, and extracted Dana through the freight elevator. The red thread is from a military-grade tactical jacket — the contractor's.

suspects.name: Richard Voss
suspects.personality: Patrician calm. Speaks in complete, deliberate sentences. Treats interrogation like a board meeting. Cracks only when confronted with specific financial data.
suspects.alibi: Claims he was at a charity dinner at the Fairmont Hotel all evening — has ticket stub and claims 30 witnesses.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Nexus Maritime was Dana's highest-profile audit target. He met her once at a compliance meeting.
suspects.motive_hint: The fraud case would have destroyed him and three others.
secrets: His charity dinner alibi has a 90-minute gap — he slipped out between 8:30pm and 10pm
He served in military intelligence before finance — knows how to hire off-books operatives
He remotely authorised the laptop wipe using a back-door admin account his IT team installed
The red tactical jacket belongs to ex-contractor Kyle Merritt, whom Voss paid in untraceable crypto

suspects.name: Sylvia Okafor
suspects.personality: Sharp, ambitious, occasionally cruel. Doesn't suffer fools. Has a tell: she tugs her earring when evading.
suspects.alibi: Was at a networking dinner, documented. Left around 9pm. Her keycard accessed the building at 9:45pm.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Dana's boss. Had recently blocked Dana's promotion. Under pressure from Nexus Maritime to 'manage' the audit.
suspects.motive_hint: She benefited from Dana disappearing — but she lacked the operational reach.
secrets: Nexus Maritime paid her firm a £400K 'consulting retainer' — essentially a bribe to slow the audit
She deleted several of Dana's internal reports from the shared drive
She returned to the office at 9:45pm looking for Dana's work files — she found the office empty

suspects.name: Tom Lacey
suspects.personality: Defensive. Speaks in technical jargon to create distance. Noticeably uncomfortable about the laptop wipe.
suspects.alibi: Says he was home all evening — no external witness.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Installed and maintained Dana's encrypted workstation. Had full admin access.
suspects.motive_hint: He had technical access nobody else did — but he was being coerced.
secrets: Voss's IT team contacted him 6 weeks ago offering him money to install a remote admin backdoor
He refused — but the backdoor was installed anyway without his knowledge
He discovered the backdoor the morning after Dana disappeared and is terrified
He has a draft email to the FBI he hasn't sent yet

suspects.name: Dana's Husband — Curtis Morse
suspects.personality: Visibly distressed. Oscillates between grief and anger. Protective of Dana's reputation.
suspects.alibi: Home all evening with their two children. Multiple neighbours can confirm his car was in the driveway.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Husband of 14 years. Says their marriage was 'complicated lately' but refuses to elaborate.
suspects.motive_hint: He stands to collect $1.2M insurance — but his alibi is airtight.
secrets: Dana had recently begun sleeping in the guest room and had consulted a divorce attorney
Curtis knew about the fraud case and had begged Dana to walk away — he feared for her safety
Dana emailed him at 8:30pm: 'I found the last piece. It's over for Voss. Don't worry.'

suspects.name: Lena Park
suspects.personality: Eager, slightly wide-eyed. Over-explains. Desperate to be helpful to the point of suspicion.
suspects.alibi: Left the office at 6:15pm, has Uber receipt and roommate confirmation.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Dana's protégée. Worked directly under her for 2 years.
suspects.motive_hint: She knew what Dana was working on — and may have inadvertently tipped someone off.
secrets: She mentioned the audit findings to her boyfriend — who works in Nexus Maritime's legal team
She has a copy of Dana's working files saved to her personal cloud backup
The files are the key to the entire fraud case and she doesn't know their value

evidence.title: Red Thread — Window Latch
evidence.description: A single red polyester thread caught in the 11th floor window latch. Lab analysis matches a military-grade tactical fleece manufactured exclusively for private security contractors. Not available commercially.
evidence.content:
FORENSIC FIBRE ANALYSIS — Item T-003
Fibre type: Polyester-aramid blend
Colour: Signal red, commercial dye batch #FF2200
Manufacturer: TacGear Solutions Ltd — contract supply only
End users: Private security, military contractors
Conclusion: Not commercially available. Narrow supplier list.

evidence.title: Elevator Log — Button 11 from Basement
evidence.description: Building elevator logs show Button 11 pressed from the Basement level at 9:01pm — during the blackout, when elevators should have been on emergency lockdown. Someone overrode the system.
evidence.content:
ELEVATOR SYSTEM LOG — Harbor Street Tower
20:47:03 — BLACKOUT: Emergency mode engaged
20:47:04 — All cars locked to ground floor (per protocol)
21:01:22 — Car B: MANUAL OVERRIDE — Basement → Floor 11
21:03:44 — Car B: Floor 11 → Basement
21:34:11 — POWER RESTORED: System reset
Note: Override requires physical keypad access in basement shaft room.

evidence.title: Dana's Final Email — 8:30 PM
evidence.description: Recovered from Dana's sent folder before the wipe. Email to her husband at 8:30pm: 'I found the last piece. It's over for Voss. Don't worry. I'm safe.' The email was also forwarded — to an unknown encrypted address.
evidence.content:
EMAIL — Sent 20:30:17
From: d.morse@pelicananalytics.com
To: cmorse@gmail.com
BCC: [ENCRYPTED RECIPIENT — ProtonMail hash]
Subject: (no subject)

'I found the last piece. It's over for Voss. Don't worry. I'm safe. Going to lock everything up and call you from the car. Home by 10.'

Note: BCC recipient unknown. Email also forwarded to encrypted address.

evidence.title: Power Grid Failure — Engineering Report
evidence.description: The Harbor substation failure was caused by a remotely triggered relay override — not a fault. The override signal originated from a laptop connected to the grid's maintenance portal. IP address traced to a VPN exit node — same VPN provider used by Nexus Maritime's IT department.
evidence.content:
SFPUC GRID INVESTIGATION REPORT
Incident: Harbor Substation CB-7 failure
Cause: DELIBERATE — remote relay override
Signal origin: VPN exit node — AnonShield VPN
AnonShield subscriber data (subpoenaed): Nexus Maritime Group IT — account #NM-2281
Override window: 20:47–21:34 (47 minutes)
Conclusion: Intentional, targeted, technically sophisticated

evidence.title: Nexus Maritime — Fraud Reconstruction
evidence.description: Dana's partial reconstruction of the $40M fraud scheme, recovered from Lena Park's cloud backup. Shows funds routed through 6 shell companies back to a Cayman account — signatory: Richard T. Voss.
evidence.content:
PELICAN ANALYTICS — RESTRICTED WORKING FILE
Case: Nexus Maritime Audit
Analyst: D. Morse
FINDINGS SUMMARY:
- $40.2M in fabricated consulting fees (2019–2026)
- Routed through: Meridian LLC → BluePath Trading → 4 shell entities
- Final destination: Cayman account — Signatory: Richard T. Voss
STATUS: COMPLETE — ready for submission to SEC

evidence.title: Laptop Wipe — Admin Log
evidence.description: Remote wipe executed at 9:03pm via a backdoor admin account. The account was created 6 weeks ago from an IP address registered to Nexus Maritime's corporate network.
evidence.content:
FORENSIC IT ANALYSIS — Workstation DM-11
Event: Remote wipe executed 21:03:17
Method: Backdoor admin account 'sysops_bk1'
Account created: September 18, 2026
Creation IP: 185.XX.XX.XX — Nexus Maritime Corp Network
Wipe scope: COMPLETE — 7-pass DoD standard
Conclusion: Premeditated. Access established weeks in advance.
